en.name: Leukocytes in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Leukocytes (white blood cells, WBCs) are the body's nucleated immune cells circulating in blood and lymphatics, comprising several lineages (neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, eosinophils, basophils) [1]. The total leukocyte count is a core component of the complete blood count (CBC) and is used to assess infection, inflammation, immunosuppression, and haematological malignancies. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a haematology analyser as part of the CBC, on whole blood (EDTA tube). It is collected in routine biochemistry and intensive care. Results are reported as a number concentration, typically in **`10*9/L`** (NPU SI convention, equivalent to `G/L`) or in the US notation **`10*3/uL`** (numerically identical: `1 ×10⁹/L = 1 ×10³/µL`). ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the LOINC Group `40654026 / LG32857-1 — Leukocytes|Number Concentration (count/vol)|Moment in time|Blood (LOINC Group)` and pulls in all its descendants. This resolves to four standard concepts, which differ only by counting method. ### Generic (method not specified) - *3010813 / 26464-8 — Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code, returned by haematology analysers when the method is not detailed. ### Method-specific - *3000905 / 6690-2 — Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count (LOINC)*: automated impedance, optical or flow-cytometric count (most common in routine practice). - *3003282 / 804-5 — Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Manual count (LOINC)*: manual counting on a haemocytometer (used as a confirmation when the analyser flags the result, or in low-resource settings). - *3032393 / 49498-9 — Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Estimate (LOINC)*: visually estimated from a stained smear (rare). **Note on units.** Recommended unit: **`10*9/L`** (`9444 / 10*9/L — billion per liter (UCUM)`), aligned with NPU [2]. LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS` is `10*3/uL` (`8848 / 10*3/uL — thousand per microliter (UCUM)`), which is the same physical quantity in US notation. Conversion: `1 ×10⁹/L = 1 ×10³/µL` (factor 1). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the LOINC Group for leukocyte number concentration in blood and pulling in all its descendants. This naturally excludes: - **Differential subpopulations** — neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, eosinophils and basophils have their own concept sets and are mapped separately. - **Other specimens**: cord blood, fetal blood, body fluid, bone marrow, CSF, dialysis fluid, peritoneal/pleural/synovial fluid, urine, stem cell product — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Pure number fraction / percentage variants** of the differential — they describe a relative composition, not the total count. - **Aggregations** like `(Maximum value during study)` — out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "WBC", "Leukocytes", "GB" (globules blancs) or similar without specifying the method: `3010813 / 26464-8 — Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood`. By far the most common. - **Method-specific source**: if the source explicitly states "automated", "manual" or "estimate", map to the corresponding method-specific concept. Otherwise, default to `3010813 / 26464-8 — Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood` — do **not** infer the method from the analyser brand. - **Unit notation**: source systems may report `10*9/L`, `G/L`, `10*3/uL`, `K/uL`, or `/uL` (×1000). All correspond to the same physical quantity and can be converted to the recommended `10*9/L` (factor 1 with `10*3/uL`, factor 0.001 with `/uL`). - **Do not confuse with**: differential counts (neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, eosinophils, basophils), leukocyte percentages, leukocyte esterase (urine), or leukocytes on non-blood specimens — all belong to other concept sets. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 26464-8 — Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood (Part description for "Leukocytes"). Available: https://loinc.org/26464-8/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
